title: Can you use two health insurance policies for one hospital bill? Here's what experts say
summary: Many individuals hold multiple health insurance policies, such as a corporate cover and a personal plan. Claiming from both policies is permitted. Here's what policyholders must know.&nbsp;

title: What are balanced advantage funds? 5 reasons why they can help you navigate market volatility
summary: Balanced Advantage Funds can help investors manage market volatility through dynamic asset allocation between equity and debt, offering stability, tax efficiency, and disciplined investing for long term wealth creation.

title: How becoming a loan guarantor can hurt your retirement income and credit score
summary: Pensioners should be mindful when considering becoming a loan guarantor, as a borrower’s default could impact their retirement savings, credit score, and overall financial stability, potentially leading to legal obligations and added financial pressure on a limited income.

title: Is India really one of the cheapest places to retire? ₹1 cr, ₹2 cr or ₹10 cr - How much is enough?
summary: Most global retirement models assume that individuals retire around the age of 60 and need to fund 20–25 years of expenses. That assumption is quietly becoming outdated.

title: Petrol, diesel price build-up: How much tax do you pay on one litre of petrol and diesel? Here's a breakdown
summary: Despite a cut in special additional excise duty on fuel, petrol and diesel prices will not decrease at retail level, as the government absorbs costs to support oil marketing companies amid high international prices. The duty on petrol was reduced from ₹13 to ₹3 per litre and diesel to nil.

title: No more easy rate cuts: What borrowers should expect next
summary: With easy rate cuts on hold, borrowers should expect stable EMIs. The focus should be on affordable loan choices, disciplined repayment, and careful planning, as higher inflation and global uncertainties may push borrowing costs higher in the future.

title: Got a banking complaint? Follow RBI’s 3-step process before approaching Ombudsman
summary: The RBI complaint process guides customers in resolving banking grievances by first approaching regulated entities, obtaining an acknowledgement, and escalating unresolved issues to the RBI Ombudsman for resolution.

title: 10 key financial changes from 1 April 2026 that will directly impact your finances
summary: Financial and regulatory changes in April 2026 include new tax rules, higher rebates, updated TDS forms, increased banking charges, changes in fuel prices and new travel refund rules. Here are 10 financial changes to look out for.

title: Why DSP Mutual Fund's Sahil Kapoor now sees a contrarian call on equities
summary: After a sharp market correction amid the US-Israel-Iran war, DSP Mutual Fund has shifted to a bullish stance, citing extreme valuations and contrarian signals.

title: PPF vs EPF vs NPS: Which is the best retirement investment? Compare interest rates, tax benefits, tenure & more

summary: Reliance Industries Ltd. is planning a massive IPO for its telecom arm, Jio Platforms Ltd. The company is setting investment banking fees at a competitive 0.65% of the issue size. This move is in line with the National Stock Exchange of India Ltd.'s proposed fees. The Jio IPO could be India's larges

title: US stocks: Citigroup dismisses report of potential US regional bank acquisition
summary: Citigroup has denied a Bloomberg report suggesting it was considering acquiring a U.S. regional bank, stating the speculation is "baseless." The bank emphasized its sole focus remains on organic growth and executing its ongoing transformation strategy. This denial comes as Citigroup works to complet

title: Rupee crosses 94/$1 mark for the first time, ends at 94.81/$ amid volatile crude prices due to US-Iran war
summary: The Indian rupee hit a record low of 94.81 against the dollar, nearing 95, as surging crude oil prices and a potentially prolonged West Asia conflict weighed on the currency. Mild central bank intervention offered little respite, with traders now forecasting levels as weak as 97 per dollar.

title: US Stocks: Rocket Pharma shares fall 15% despite FDA nod for childhood disorder treatment
summary: Rocket Pharmaceuticals' shares dropped significantly after gaining FDA approval for Kresladi, a gene therapy for a rare childhood immune disorder. Investors are concerned about practical challenges limiting patient access, despite the drug's potential to help those with severe Leukocyte Adhesion Def

title: US stocks: US Fed's Anna Paulson says Iran war increases risks to growth, inflation
summary: Philadelphia Federal Reserve President ​Anna Paulson said on ​Friday the Iran war is creating challenges for ​the U.S. economy, while stopping short of saying what that might mean for near-term changes in monetary policy.

title: RBI mandates Legal Entity Identifier, Unique Transaction Identifier for financial mkt transactions
summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) on Friday mandated the use of Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) and Unique Transaction Identifier (UTI) for transactions in financial markets.

title: Centre plans to borrow Rs 8.20 lakh cr from market in first half of FY27
summary: The Centre is planning to mobilise Rs 8 lakh crore through dated securities during the April-September period of 2026-27 to fund the revenue gap, the finance ministry said on Friday.

title: SAEL Industries, Vishvaraj Environment, Symbiotec Pharmalab and 3 others get Sebi nod to launch IPO
summary: Securities and Exchange Board of India approved IPOs of six companies, including Vishvaraj Environment and SAEL Industries. Firms span sectors like renewable energy, chemicals and SaaS, while Jindal Supreme withdrew its draft papers, reflecting active primary market momentum.

title: Global Stocks: Chinese EV maker BYD posts first annual profit decline in four years
summary: BYD , China's ​biggest electric ​vehicle maker by ​sales, on Friday posted its first annual profit drop in ‌four ⁠years, hit ⁠by weak sales in ​the world's largest auto market.

title: US Stocks: SpaceX's listing stirs up social media frenzy, ticker bets
summary: From rocket launches drawing millions of YouTube views to social media frenzy over its ​potential listing, SpaceX's debut is shaping up to ​be a landmark moment for Wall Street.
